summary: All test files have meaningful assertions that verify actual behavior. 50+ new test assertions covering BTC parsing, EVM parsing, ViewModel merge/sort, formatting helpers, and error handling. Tests follow TDD contract-testing pattern. Good coverage of edge cases (invalid numeric values, null timestamps, zero confirmations, partial failures).

findings.issue: testParseBlockcypherTimestamp_validFormat uses a range check instead of exact value assertion due to timezone sensitivity in SimpleDateFormat. This is a pragmatic workaround. Consider using java.time.Instant for more predictable ISO 8601 parsing in the production code, but this is a separate improvement.
findings.suggestion: Acceptable as-is. Range check validates the core parsing logic.

findings.issue: fetchTransactionHistory integration tests use positional any() matchers instead of named parameter eq() matchers because Mockito has issues with Kotlin suspend functions and default parameters. This means the mock matches ANY call to getTransactions, which is acceptable for these unit tests since there is only one call per test.
